name: Paella (New York Times Cook Book)
recipeIngredient: 2 oz ham, cut in thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove meat from the lobster.
2. Shell and devein the shrimp.
3. Scrub mussels and clams.
4. Cut chicken into medium-sized serving pieces.
5. Combine oregano, peppercorns, garlic, salt, 2 tbsp olive oil, and vinegar. Mash with the back of a spoon or mortar.
6. Rub chicken with the mixture.
7. Heat remaining olive oil in a deep, heavy skillet. Brown chicken lightly over moderate heat.
8. Add ham, chorizo, salt pork, onion, green pepper, coriander, and capers. Cook 10 minutes over low heat.
9. Add tomato sauce and rice. Cook 5 minutes.
10. Add boiling water, saffron, and shrimp. Mix well and cook rapidly, covered, until liquid is absorbed, about 20 minutes.
11. Turn rice from top to bottom with a large spoon.
12. Add lobster meat and peas; cover and cook 5 minutes longer.
13. Steam mussels and clams in a little water until their shells open.
14. Heat pimientos and drain.
15. Use the mussels, clams, and pimientos as a garnish.
